title: Happiness Dystopia
html:
TL;DR

  • Speculative essay/short-fiction set in a near-future where loneliness is the leading disease and the government rolls out a "happiness score" app (integrated into brain chips for the privileged) to combat it
  • Core mechanic: points awarded for time physically spent around other people, regardless of quality — a Goodhart-fail by design
  • Three classes of opt-outs: the failed (cautionary tale), the secret elite who run the game (Joe Flom-types in bubbles), and the rare hippie liberated cohort
  • Recurring Yahir-thesis embedded: "democratic states give the illusion of choice to make the population fall in line" — same lens as 2022 03 11 made people vote to prove voting is a farce only effective t
  • Direct lineage to Ready Player One vertical-stacking imagery; conceptual neighbor of surveillance state and mindless human interface disruption

Notes

Written October 2022, this is one of Yahir's longer pieces of speculative fiction — almost a treatment for a short story or essay-essay hybrid. The premise: in a vertically-stacked future society where loneliness is epidemic, a state-mandated happiness score app turns proximity-to-other-humans into a measurable currency, with social class flowing from cumulative point totals.

The structural argument is that the system is designed to be a carrot on a stick — the unattainable concept of "true happiness" keeps people grinding at a metric they vaguely sense is fake but can't afford to opt out of. Yahir maps three opt-out tiers: (1) the social outcasts at the bottom that everyone else uses as a fear-anchor (drawing an explicit South-justifying-slavery analogy), (2) a hidden elite "Joe Flom-types" who govern the game from outside it, and (3) the rare hippies who said "fuck u" and live primitive-but-free.

The closing aside — "democratic states are simply giving the illusion to population that they have choice, which makes them fall in line" — is the same Marxist-skeptical-of-democracy thread that runs through 2021 12 04 capitalisms climate, 2022 03 11 made people vote to prove voting is a farce only effective t, and the violence as only effective tool concept. This is Yahir's social control cluster surfacing in fiction-form.
